title: Dividends, Buybacks Lift Union Pacific (UNP) Despite Cost Woes
text: Supply-chain woes, high debt load and escalated operating expenses hurt the Union Pacific (UNP) stock.

title: Improved Valuation Makes Union Pacific Worth Considering
text: UNP has fallen 27% from the 12-month high at the end of March. Labor issues, fuel costs, and inflation have posed challenges.

title: Union Pacific Corporation Announces Third Quarter 2022 Earnings Release Date
text: OMAHA, Neb. , Sept. 22, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Union Pacific Corporation (NYSE: UNP) will release third quarter 2022 financial and operating results on Thursday, Oct. 20, 2022, at 7:45 a.m.

title: Railroads vs. unions: What's next and why the strike threat isn't over
text: The threat of a strike from freight railroad unions isn't over. One union has already rejected the tentative deal and said it could strike at the end of the month.

title: Three Simple Things to Teach Your Loved Ones During National Rail Safety Week
text: Union Pacific Railroad, Safe Kids Worldwide and Animated TV Series, 'Chuggington,' Team Up to Share Lifesaving Rail Safety Messages OMAHA, Neb. , Sept. 19, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- The U.S. freight rail network is made up of nearly 140,000 miles of track, traversing serene landscapes and busy cities.

title: Biden to ‘blame' for what could've been a ‘disastrous' railroad strike: Freedom Foundation CEO
text: Freedom Foundation CEO Aaron Withe blasted Biden's economy and "poor working conditions" for railroad workers as the cause of the now-averted labor strike conflict.

title: U.S. railroad worker fight for pay, benefits could be model for other deals
text: Union railworkers in the United States scored a potential key victory in their fight for improved pay and working conditions on Thursday in what could be a model for other unions.

title: Class I railroads work to quickly resume operations
text: The U.S. operations of the Class I railroads are working to resume operations “immediately” now that a deal has been reached to avoid a strike by union members on Friday. Two of the largest labor unions — those representing locomotive engineers and train conductors — reached an eleventh-hour tentative agreement with the railroads.

title: Workers Say Railroads' Efficiency Push Became Too Much
text: Employees say the inflexibility of scheduling upended their personal lives. The companies say they maintained service while using fewer resources.

title: Rail Strike, 'Significant Damage' To Economy Derailed, Biden Announces
text: A rail strike which could have brought key shipments to a standstill, straining the high-inflation economy, seems to have been averted. The post Rail Strike, 'Significant Damage' To Economy Derailed, Biden Announces appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.

title: Railroads and unions reach tentative agreement by addressing key attendance policy
text: Freight railroad unions and management reached a tentative deal to avert a strike, addressing a key attendance policy that had been holding up the deal.

title: US stocks fall as jobless claims drop and railroad union deal averts strike
text: Weekly jobless claims fell for a fifth straight week last week to 213,000, below economist estimates for 227,000 in jobless claims.

title: 2 Railroad Stocks in Focus After Tentative Union Deal
text: Railroad stocks are mixed this morning, following news that major U.S. railroad companies and unions reached a tentative deal last night, following 20 hours of talks, to stop workers from striking.

title: Rail unions have reached a tentative deal to avert economy-rattling strike
text: After 20 hours of talks, the White House helped avert a strike that would've cost $2 billion a day. Rail workers will get better pay and conditions.

title: Union Pacific and CSX Stocks Jump as Railroads Reach Tentative Pact
text: Railroad stocks are rising Thursday after companies and union negotiators reach a tentative contract agreement.

title: Railroads wind down some operations; White House eyes options to move freight
text: The U.S.-based Class I railroads have been taking steps this week to prepare for a potential strike by union members after midnight Friday by embargoing security-sensitive material and even winding down operations for intermodal and temperature-controlled rail traffic. On Wednesday, Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) will close all its gates to intermodal traffic at 5 p.m.

title: U.S. railroad strike hinges on dispute over attendance policies
text: One of the labor unions representing U.S. railroad workers said Wednesday its members rejected a tentative agreement its leaders had reached, a setback in efforts to avoid a potential strike that would disrupt the movement of goods across the country.

title: Railroad stocks fall, help drag Dow transports down in an up market
text: Shares of railroad operators fell Wednesday, helping drag the Dow Jones Transportation Average DJT, -1.02% down by 1.3% in midday trading despite a 39-point gain in the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA, +0.13% and 0.3% rise in the S&P 500 SPX, +0.29% , amid concerns over a possible labor strike. Shares of Union Pacific Corp. UNP, -4.53% shed 4.8%, Norfolk Southern Corp. NSC, -2.74% dropped 2.8% and CSX Corp. CSX, -2.42% slumped 2.6%.

title: US railways to halt grain shipments ahead of potential shutdown, agriculture sources
text: U.S. railroads will start halting crop shipments on Thursday, a day ahead of a potential work stoppage. Railroads have until Friday to reach deal with holdout unions.

title: Railroads say they won't lock out workers as negotiators meet with Labor Secretary Walsh
text: Railroads have already started diverting freight. A strike could cost the U.S. economy more than $2 billion a day.

title: Rail Strike Could Start Friday, Just What The High-Inflation, Low-Growth Economy Doesn't Need
text: A rail workers strike could start Friday, bringing key shipments to a standstill, straining a low-growth, high-inflation economy. The post Rail Strike Could Start Friday, Just What The High-Inflation, Low-Growth Economy Doesn't Need appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.

title: Threat of Work Stoppage by Railroads Looms Large: An Analysis
text: Operations by railroads like UNP, NSC and CSX will be disrupted if the strike materializes. This, in turn, will deal a massive blow to the economy.

title: Railroads set to halt transport of hazardous materials ahead of strike deadline
text: Freight railroads said they are planning to halt the transport of hazardous materials and sensitive cargoes, such as chemicals used in fertilizer and chlorine for water purification, as they continue negotiations with two labor unions.

title: Railroad Dispute: White House Tries To Avert $2 Billion A Day Transportation Strike
text: Biden administration officials have begun to prepare for a strike that could shut down thousands of freight trains as well as disrupt passenger operations.

title: Union Pacific Issues $600 Million in Green Bonds to Fund Investments Aimed at Reducing Carbon Footprint
text: OMAHA, Neb. , Sept. 12, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Union Pacific Corporation (NYSE: UNP) issued a $600 million green bond offering to fund investments aimed at decarbonizing the company's footprint and achieving set emissions reduction targets.

title: Some Class I railroads optimistic about volume growth, hiring initiatives
text: U.S. Class I railroads remain optimistic that hiring initiatives and operational changes should bring about improved service for the remainder of the year and into 2023, according to top executives' remarks at a recent investor conference. “We're in pretty smooth sailing for the rest of the fall, and I expect continuous improvement in terms of increasing car velocity, improved utilization of crews and improved car-per-carload metrics,” said Union Pacific (NYSE: UNP) President and CEO Lance Fritz at a recent conference sponsored by investment firm Cowen.

title: 2 Railroad Stocks With Impressive Dividend Yield to Watch
text: Investors should keep tabs on dividend-growth railroad stocks like Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC).

I'm your host, Jason Seidl.

title: West Coast port congestion leads Norfolk Southern, Union Pacific to test new ideas in cross-country rail shipping
text: There's a new way ports, ocean carriers and rails are move shipping containers across the U.S. as a result of West Coast port congestion.

title: What's Happening With Union Pacific Stock?
text: Union Pacific stock (NYSE: UNP) has seen an 11% fall this year, performing slightly better than the broader S&P500 index, which is down 17%.
